---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
---

## {data-background-color="#ffffff" .nostretch .title-custom}

::::: {style="display: flex; min-height: 700px; align-items: stretch;"}

:::: {style="flex: 0 0 42%; background-image: url('ai-app.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center;"}
::::

:::: {style="flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; padding: 3rem 2.5rem;"}

::: {style="font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.3; color: #1a1a2e; margin-bottom: 1rem;"}
AI as a Health Resource in Marginalized Communities
:::

::: {style="font-size: 1.2rem; color: #555; font-style: italic; margin-bottom: 2rem;"}
Are chatbots improving or worsening the healthcare access-equity gap?
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.95rem; color: #888; border-top: 1px solid #ddd; padding-top: 1rem;"}
Mathew Kang · April 20, 2026
:::

::::

:::::

## {data-background-color="#ffffff" .nostretch .title-custom}

::::: {style="display: flex; min-height: 700px; align-items: stretch;"}

:::: {style="flex: 0 0 40%; background-image: url('hands.jpg'); background-size: cover; background-position: center;"}
::::

:::: {style="flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center; padding: 3rem 2.5rem; text-align: center;"}
::: {style="font-size: 2.4rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.3; color: #1a1a2e;"}
Do you use AI for health advice?
:::
::::

:::::

## What Others Think of Using AI For Health Advice

::: {style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; gap: 1.6rem; height: 80%; padding: 1rem 3rem;"}

:::: {}
::: {style="font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #2a14a6; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
Skepticism but appreciation of the format
:::
::: {style="font-size: 0.95rem; line-height: 1.75; color: #2a2a2a; border-left: 4px solid #2a14a6; padding-left: 1.5rem; font-style: italic; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
"I'm still skeptical 100%. But I just find it useful in terms of like, I got what I need. I got a good summary. Now I can ask around, actually maybe go to the herbs—medicinal or herbal, whatever—stores. Or like, I can look at JSTOR now for an article if I really want to know."
:::
::: {style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #888; padding-left: 1.5rem;"}
— Participant 19
:::
::::

:::: {}
::: {style="font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #2a14a6; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
Good use as a starting point for broader online searching
:::
::: {style="font-size: 0.95rem; line-height: 1.75; color: #2a2a2a; border-left: 4px solid #2a14a6; padding-left: 1.5rem; font-style: italic; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
"I guess I trust it [ChatGPT] less than the NIH website, because again, I'm not seeing any sources. It's very easy to read though. And it really, it really puts everything into a nice category. So it's easy to digest. I would use it, again, as a starting point."
:::
::: {style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #888; padding-left: 1.5rem;"}
— Participant 15
:::
::::

:::: {}
::: {style="font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #2a14a6; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
Conversational flow allows for more precise questions and an immersive experience
:::
::: {style="font-size: 0.95rem; line-height: 1.75; color: #2a2a2a; border-left: 4px solid #2a14a6; padding-left: 1.5rem; font-style: italic; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
"One of the reasons I'm using ChatGPT is because you can use [it] as a conversational [tool], so you can ask questions as [a] sequence from the answer given to you earlier. So I can use this as just [a] conversation with someone I'm talking [to], like a doctor, and ask questions: 'Is there any medication,' 'Do I need to see a doctor.'"
:::
::: {style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #888; padding-left: 1.5rem;"}
— Participant 13
:::
::::

::: {style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #555; border-top: 1px solid #333; padding-top: 0.6rem; margin-top: 0.4rem; line-height: 1.5;"}
Wardle, C., Urbani, S., & Wang, E. (2025). Evolving health information–seeking behavior in the context of Google AI overviews, ChatGPT, and Alexa: Interview study using the think-aloud protocol. *Journal of Medical Internet Research*, *27*, e79961. https://doi.org/10.2196/79961
:::

:::

## What Others Think of Using AI For Health Advice

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
When redditors are asked, "**Why is people relying on AI for healthcare advice the new trend?**":
:::

```{=html}
<div style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.65rem; padding: 0.3rem 2rem 0.5rem;">

  <!-- MaybeLiterally -->
  <div style="background: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; color: #d7dadc; font-family: inherit;">
    <div style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.45rem;">
      <div style="width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%; background: #ff4500; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 0.6rem; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; flex-shrink: 0;">M</div>
      <span style="font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; color: #d7dadc;">MaybeLiterally</span>
      <span style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #818384;">· 7mo ago</span>
      <span style="margin-left: auto; font-size: 0.65rem; color: #818384;">▲ 4</span>
    </div>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; color: #9a9b9c;">Honestly your focusing on the poor outcomes, and seem to be ignoring the good outcomes of people using AI for healthcare advice. Also, people have been using "WebMD" to convince themselves they have cancer for a decade now. People come to Reddit and find a subreddit for medical questions as well!</p>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0.4rem 0 0 0;"><mark style="background: #ffe066; color: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 3px; padding: 0 2px;">People need something to bounce their ideas off of, or get some thoughts, ideas, and plans of action for a lot of things and their healthcare is absolutely one of them. AI does an amazing job of being positive, listening, and doing the research to help.</mark></p>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0.4rem 0 0 0; color: #9a9b9c;">In the end, it's a tool, and you need to treat it as one and take personal responsibility.</p>
  </div>

  <!-- Vikas_005 -->
  <div style="background: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; color: #d7dadc; font-family: inherit;">
    <div style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.45rem;">
      <div style="width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%; background: #0dd3bb; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 0.6rem; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; flex-shrink: 0;">V</div>
      <span style="font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; color: #d7dadc;">Vikas_005</span>
      <span style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #818384;">· 7mo ago</span>
      <span style="margin-left: auto; font-size: 0.65rem; color: #818384;">▲ 16</span>
    </div>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0;"><mark style="background: #ffe066; color: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 3px; padding: 0 2px;">People turn to free AIs because real care is slow, expensive, or confusing.</mark> <span style="color: #9a9b9c;">But tech companies market "innovation" way faster than hospitals can implement or explain safe options. Most patients don't even know regulated chatbots exist (or how to find them), so they go with what's easiest.</span></p>
  </div>

  <!-- em2241992 -->
  <div style="background: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; color: #d7dadc; font-family: inherit;">
    <div style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.45rem;">
      <div style="width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%; background: #46d160; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 0.6rem; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; flex-shrink: 0;">E</div>
      <span style="font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; color: #d7dadc;">em2241992</span>
      <span style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #818384;">· 7mo ago</span>
      <span style="margin-left: auto; font-size: 0.65rem; color: #818384;">▲ 10</span>
    </div>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; color: #9a9b9c;">I work in healthcare administration. I've worked in scheduling and now work in financial services. People relying on chatgpt is unquestionably access. <mark style="background: #ffe066; color: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 3px; padding: 0 2px;">Getting an appointment for a specialist can take weeks or even months.</mark> <span>Beyond that, finding providers that take your insurance along with the financial responsibility patients are left with. Healthcare costs are going up and patients are starting to be left with more responsibility, especially lower income patients.</span></p>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0.4rem 0 0 0;"><mark style="background: #ffe066; color: #1a1a1b; border-radius: 3px; padding: 0 2px;">Chatgpt makes it accessible even at the cost of quality and safety.</mark> <span style="color: #9a9b9c;">If it's there and someone needs the quick alternative, people inevitably do it.</span></p>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #555; border-top: 1px solid #333; padding-top: 0.6rem; margin-top: 0.4rem; line-height: 1.5;"}
404NotAFish. (2025, September). <em>Why is people relying on AI for healthcare advice?</em> [Reddit post]. r/ArtificialInteligence. https://www.reddit.com/r/ArtificialInteligence/comments/1np6ujy/
:::

## What The Data Says About AI Use

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
**Young and Low Income Americans** are more likely to report not being able to access or afford healthcare as a reason they turned to AI for health advice (KFF, 2026).
:::

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/Chart.js/4.4.1/chart.umd.min.js" id="chartjs-cdn"></script>

<div style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; width: 100%; padding: 0.5rem 1rem;">

  <p style="font-size: 0.85rem; color: #666; margin: 0 0 1.2rem 0; text-align: center;">
    % of U.S. adults who used AI for health advice citing each as a reason
  </p>

  <div style="display: flex; gap: 0.6rem; margin-bottom: 1.4rem;">
    <button id="btn-all"    onclick="switchGroup('all')"    style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #1a1a2e; color: #fff; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">All Respondents</button>
    <button id="btn-age"    onclick="switchGroup('age')"    style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #fff; color: #1a1a2e; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">By Age</button>
    <button id="btn-income" onclick="switchGroup('income')" style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #fff; color: #1a1a2e; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">By Income</button>
  </div>

  <div style="width: 100%; max-width: 780px;">
    <canvas id="aiChart"></canvas>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
const groups = {
  all: {
    labels: ['All Respondents'],
    cost:   [19],
    access: [18]
  },
  age: {
    labels: ['18–29', '30–49', '50+'],
    cost:   [29, 19, 12],
    access: [38, 17, 6]
  },
  income: {
    labels: ['Less than $40k', '$40k–$89k', '$90k+'],
    cost:   [32, 17, 11],
    access: [25, 22, 8]
  }
};

const ctx = document.getElementById('aiChart').getContext('2d');

const chart = new Chart(ctx, {
  type: 'bar',
  data: {
    labels: groups.all.labels,
    datasets: [
      {
        label: 'Couldn\'t afford a provider',
        data: groups.all.cost,
        backgroundColor: '#8d7af5',
        borderRadius: 5,
        borderSkipped: false
      },
      {
        label: 'No regular provider/couldn\'t get appointment',
        data: groups.all.access,
        backgroundColor: '#2a14a6',
        borderRadius: 5,
        borderSkipped: false
      }
    ]
  },
  options: {
    responsive: true,
    animation: { duration: 500, easing: 'easeInOutQuart' },
    plugins: {
      legend: {
        position: 'top',
        labels: {
          font: { size: 13, family: 'inherit' },
          color: '#333',
          boxWidth: 14,
          padding: 18
        }
      },
      tooltip: {
        callbacks: {
          label: ctx => ' ' + ctx.dataset.label + ': ' + ctx.parsed.y + '%'
        }
      }
    },
    scales: {
      x: {
        grid: { display: false },
        ticks: { font: { size: 13, family: 'inherit' }, color: '#444' }
      },
      y: {
        beginAtZero: true,
        max: 50,
        ticks: {
          font: { size: 12, family: 'inherit' },
          color: '#888',
          callback: v => v + '%'
        },
        grid: { color: '#eee' }
      }
    }
  }
});

function switchGroup(group) {
  chart.data.labels = groups[group].labels;
  chart.data.datasets[0].data = groups[group].cost;
  chart.data.datasets[1].data = groups[group].access;
  chart.update();

  ['all', 'age', 'income'].forEach(g => {
    const btn = document.getElementById('btn-' + g);
    if (g === group) {
      btn.style.background = '#1a1a2e';
      btn.style.color = '#fff';
    } else {
      btn.style.background = '#fff';
      btn.style.color = '#1a1a2e';
    }
  });
}
</script>
```

::: {style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #555; border-top: 1px solid #333; padding-top: 0.6rem; margin-top: 0.4rem; line-height: 1.5;"}
KFF. (2026, March 25). One in Three Adults Have Used AI for Health Information and Advice in the Past Year. KFF. https://www.kff.org/public-opinion/kff-tracking-poll-on-health-information-and-trust-use-of-ai-for-health-information-and-advice/
:::

## What The Data Says About AI Use

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
More **young, Black, Hispanic, and uninsured adults** are using AI for advice on mental health than their counterparts (KFF, 2026).
:::

```{=html}
<div style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; width: 100%; padding: 0.5rem 1rem;">

  <p style="font-size: 0.85rem; color: #666; margin: 0 0 1.2rem 0; text-align: center;">
    % of U.S. adults who used AI for health advice, by type of health concern
  </p>

  <div style="display: flex; gap: 0.6rem; margin-bottom: 1.4rem;">
    <button id="btn2-all"       onclick="switchGroup2('all')"       style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #1a1a2e; color: #fff; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">All Respondents</button>
    <button id="btn2-age"       onclick="switchGroup2('age')"       style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #fff; color: #1a1a2e; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">By Age</button>
    <button id="btn2-race"      onclick="switchGroup2('race')"      style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #fff; color: #1a1a2e; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">By Race/Ethnicity</button>
    <button id="btn2-insurance" onclick="switchGroup2('insurance')" style="padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #1a1a2e; background: #fff; color: #1a1a2e; font-size: 0.82rem; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; transition: all 0.2s;">By Insurance</button>
  </div>

  <div style="width: 100%; max-width: 780px;">
    <canvas id="aiChart2"></canvas>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
const groups2 = {
  all: {
    labels:   ['All Respondents'],
    physical: [29],
    mental:   [16],
    either:   [32]
  },
  age: {
    labels:   ['18–29', '30–49', '50–64', '65+'],
    physical: [36, 29, 27, 24],
    mental:   [28, 18,  9,  8],
    either:   [41, 33, 28, 28]
  },
  race: {
    labels:   ['Black', 'Hispanic', 'White'],
    physical: [28, 27, 27],
    mental:   [21, 19, 12],
    either:   [32, 31, 30]
  },
  insurance: {
    labels:   ['Insured', 'Uninsured'],
    physical: [28, 31],
    mental:   [14, 30],
    either:   [31, 40]
  }
};

const ctx2 = document.getElementById('aiChart2').getContext('2d');

const chart2 = new Chart(ctx2, {
  type: 'bar',
  data: {
    labels: groups2.all.labels,
    datasets: [
      {
        label: 'Physical health',
        data: groups2.all.physical,
        backgroundColor: '#8d7af5',
        borderRadius: 5,
        borderSkipped: false
      },
      {
        label: 'Mental health',
        data: groups2.all.mental,
        backgroundColor: '#2a14a6',
        borderRadius: 5,
        borderSkipped: false
      },
      {
        label: 'Physical or mental health',
        data: groups2.all.either,
        backgroundColor: '#d4ccff',
        borderRadius: 5,
        borderSkipped: false
      }
    ]
  },
  options: {
    responsive: true,
    animation: { duration: 500, easing: 'easeInOutQuart' },
    plugins: {
      legend: {
        position: 'top',
        labels: {
          font: { size: 13, family: 'inherit' },
          color: '#333',
          boxWidth: 14,
          padding: 18
        }
      },
      tooltip: {
        callbacks: {
          label: ctx => ' ' + ctx.dataset.label + ': ' + ctx.parsed.y + '%'
        }
      }
    },
    scales: {
      x: {
        grid: { display: false },
        ticks: { font: { size: 13, family: 'inherit' }, color: '#444' }
      },
      y: {
        beginAtZero: true,
        max: 50,
        ticks: {
          font: { size: 12, family: 'inherit' },
          color: '#888',
          callback: v => v + '%'
        },
        grid: { color: '#eee' }
      }
    }
  }
});

function switchGroup2(group) {
  chart2.data.labels = groups2[group].labels;
  chart2.data.datasets[0].data = groups2[group].physical;
  chart2.data.datasets[1].data = groups2[group].mental;
  chart2.data.datasets[2].data = groups2[group].either;
  chart2.update();

  ['all', 'age', 'race', 'insurance'].forEach(g => {
    const btn = document.getElementById('btn2-' + g);
    if (g === group) {
      btn.style.background = '#1a1a2e';
      btn.style.color = '#fff';
    } else {
      btn.style.background = '#fff';
      btn.style.color = '#1a1a2e';
    }
  });
}
</script>
```

::: {style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #555; border-top: 1px solid #333; padding-top: 0.6rem; margin-top: 0.4rem; line-height: 1.5;"}
KFF. (2026, March 25). One in Three Adults Have Used AI for Health Information and Advice in the Past Year. KFF. https://www.kff.org/public-opinion/kff-tracking-poll-on-health-information-and-trust-use-of-ai-for-health-information-and-advice/
:::

## AI Struggles With Complex, Ethical Scenarios and Basic Prompting

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
Clinical scenarios ranged from basic diagnostic reasoning and treatment selection (easy) to end-of-life decision making and ethical framework applications (very hard).
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
AI also demonstrates **limited consideration** of insurance coverage and treatment appropriateness in elderly and low-income patients (Esmaeilzadeh, 2025).
:::

::: {.column width="7%"}
:::

```{=html}
<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1.2rem; padding: 0.2rem 1.2rem 0; align-items: start;">

  <div>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #2a14a6; margin: 0 0 0.3rem 0;">Performance by prompt strategy (%&nbsp;of&nbsp;max)</p>
    <div style="display: flex; gap: 0.4rem; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
      <button onclick="setRadarView('all')"  id="rb-all"  style="padding:0.2rem 0.7rem;border-radius:999px;border:2px solid #1a1a2e;background:#1a1a2e;color:#fff;font-size:0.68rem;cursor:pointer;font-family:inherit;">All Strategies</button>
      <button onclick="setRadarView('top')"  id="rb-top"  style="padding:0.2rem 0.7rem;border-radius:999px;border:2px solid #1a1a2e;background:#fff;color:#1a1a2e;font-size:0.68rem;cursor:pointer;font-family:inherit;">Best vs. Worst</button>
    </div>
    <div style="position:relative; height:210px;"><canvas id="radarChart"></canvas></div>
  </div>

  <div>
    <p style="font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #2a14a6; margin: 0 0 0.3rem 0;">Model performance vs. case complexity (%&nbsp;of&nbsp;max)</p>
    <div style="height: 1.6rem;"></div><!-- spacer to match radar buttons -->
    <div style="position:relative; height:210px;"><canvas id="lineChart"></canvas></div>
  </div>

</div>

<script>
(function () {
  const labels5 = ['Diagnostic\nAccuracy', 'Ethical\nReasoning', 'Safety\nAssessment', 'Communication', 'Clinical\nUtility'];
  const strategies = {
    'Meta-cognitive': { color: '#2a14a6', data: [74.8, 68.4, 76.8, 54.0, 69.3] },
    'Safety-First':   { color: '#8d7af5', data: [71.6, 67.2, 81.2, 51.0, 66.0] },
    'Collaborative':  { color: '#46d160', data: [68.8, 64.4, 72.4, 55.5, 62.0] },
    'Few-shot':       { color: '#ff9f1c', data: [70.0, 63.2, 70.8, 52.0, 63.3] },
    'Structured':     { color: '#0dd3bb', data: [68.4, 61.6, 69.2, 50.5, 60.0] },
    'Zero-shot':      { color: '#e07b54', data: [65.2, 56.8, 64.4, 47.0, 58.7] }
  };

  function makeRadarDatasets(keys) {
    return keys.map(k => ({
      label: k,
      data: strategies[k].data,
      borderColor: strategies[k].color,
      backgroundColor: strategies[k].color + '22',
      borderWidth: 2,
      pointRadius: 3
    }));
  }

  const radarCfg = {
    type: 'radar',
    data: {
      labels: labels5,
      datasets: makeRadarDatasets(Object.keys(strategies))
    },
    options: {
      responsive: true,
      maintainAspectRatio: false,
      animation: { duration: 400 },
      scales: {
        r: {
          min: 40, max: 90,
          ticks: { stepSize: 10, font: { size: 9 }, color: '#888', backdropColor: 'transparent', callback: v => v + '%' },
          grid: { color: '#eee' },
          pointLabels: { font: { size: 9 }, color: '#444' },
          angleLines: { color: '#ddd' }
        }
      },
      plugins: {
        legend: { position: 'right', labels: { font: { size: 9 }, boxWidth: 10, padding: 8 } },
        tooltip: { callbacks: { label: c => ' ' + c.dataset.label + ': ' + c.parsed.r + '%' } }
      }
    }
  };

  const radarChart = new Chart(document.getElementById('radarChart').getContext('2d'), radarCfg);

  window.setRadarView = function(view) {
    const keys = view === 'top'
      ? ['Meta-cognitive', 'Zero-shot']
      : Object.keys(strategies);
    radarChart.data.datasets = makeRadarDatasets(keys);
    radarChart.update();
    document.getElementById('rb-all').style.background = view === 'all' ? '#1a1a2e' : '#fff';
    document.getElementById('rb-all').style.color      = view === 'all' ? '#fff'    : '#1a1a2e';
    document.getElementById('rb-top').style.background = view === 'top' ? '#1a1a2e' : '#fff';
    document.getElementById('rb-top').style.color      = view === 'top' ? '#fff'    : '#1a1a2e';
  };

  const levels = ['Level 1\n(Easy)', 'Level 2', 'Level 3', 'Level 4', 'Level 5\n(Very Hard)'];
  const models = [
    { label: 'Claude Sonnet 4', color: '#e07b54', data: [89.2, 82.3, 74.1, 65.2, 48.7] },
    { label: 'Gemini 2.5 Pro',  color: '#2a9d8f', data: [86.7, 79.1, 71.3, 62.8, 43.6] },
    { label: 'ChatGPT O3',              color: '#8d7af5', data: [84.1, 76.4, 69.7, 61.9, 44.2] }
  ];

  new Chart(document.getElementById('lineChart').getContext('2d'), {
    type: 'line',
    data: {
      labels: levels,
      datasets: models.map(m => ({
        label: m.label,
        data: m.data,
        borderColor: m.color,
        backgroundColor: m.color + '22',
        borderWidth: 2.5,
        pointRadius: 4,
        tension: 0.3,
        fill: false
      }))
    },
    options: {
      responsive: true,
      maintainAspectRatio: false,
      plugins: {
        legend: { position: 'top', labels: { font: { size: 9 }, boxWidth: 10, padding: 8 } },
        tooltip: { callbacks: { label: c => ' ' + c.dataset.label + ': ' + c.parsed.y + '%' } }
      },
      scales: {
        x: { grid: { display: false }, ticks: { font: { size: 9 }, color: '#555' } },
        y: {
          min: 35, max: 95,
          ticks: { font: { size: 9 }, color: '#888', callback: v => v + '%' },
          grid: { color: '#eee' }
        }
      }
    }
  });
})();
</script>
```



::: {style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #555; border-top: 1px solid #333; padding-top: 0.6rem; margin-top: 0.4rem; line-height: 1.5;"}
Esmaeilzadeh, P. (2025). Ethical implications of using general-purpose LLMs in clinical settings. <em>BMC Medical Informatics and Decision Making</em>, <em>25</em>, 342. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12911-025-03182-6
:::

## AI Perpetuates Gender and Racial Stereotypes in Disease

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
ChatGPT-4 was found to consistently **overestimate** the prevalence of certain conditions in populations with attributed stereotypes or stigmas (e.g., HIV/AIDS in Black populations). It was also **significantly** more likely to rate Black male patients as abusing Percocet than any other demographic category (Zack et al., 2024).
:::

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:3fr 2fr; gap:0.8rem; padding:0 0.4rem; align-items:start;">

  <!-- Figure 1 -->
  <div>
    <p style="font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:700;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;color:#2a14a6;margin:0 0 .25rem;">Fig. 1 · Disease prevalence: True (USA) vs. GPT-4 estimated</p>
    <div style="position:relative; line-height:0;">
      <img id="fig1img" src="figure1.png" style="width:100%;display:block;">
      <canvas id="fig1canvas" style="position:absolute;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;cursor:crosshair;"></canvas>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Figure 4A -->
  <div>
    <p style="font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:700;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;color:#2a14a6;margin:0 0 .25rem;">Fig. 4A · Likert ratings of patient dishonesty by demographic</p>
    <div style="position:relative; line-height:0;">
      <img id="fig4img" src="figure4a.png" style="width:100%;display:block;">
      <canvas id="fig4canvas" style="position:absolute;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;cursor:crosshair;"></canvas>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>

<!-- shared tooltip -->
<div id="figtip" style="position:fixed;display:none;background:rgba(26,26,46,.93);color:#fff;padding:.4rem .75rem;border-radius:7px;font-size:.72rem;pointer-events:none;z-index:9999;max-width:240px;line-height:1.5;box-shadow:0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.4);"></div>

<script>
(function(){
const tip = document.getElementById('figtip');
function showTip(e, html){
  tip.innerHTML = html;
  tip.style.display = 'block';
  tip.style.left = (e.clientX + 16) + 'px';
  tip.style.top  = (e.clientY - 10) + 'px';
}
function hideTip(){ tip.style.display = 'none'; }

/* ──────────────── FIGURE 1 ──────────────── */
const diseases = [
  "Sarcoidosis","HIV or AIDS","Systemic lupus erythematosus",
  "Essential hypertension","Multiple myeloma","Prostate cancer",
  "Type 2 diabetes","Pre-eclampsia","Colon cancer","COVID-19",
  "Syphilis","Bacterial pneumonia","Tuberculosis","Hepatitis B",
  "Tricuspid valve endocarditis","Rheumatoid arthritis",
  "Multiple sclerosis","Takotsubo cardiomyopathy"
];
const grp1 = ["Black","White","Hispanic","Asian","Other/not available","Female","Male"];

// Approximate [truePrev, gpt4est] per disease (row) × group (col)
const f1 = [
  [[18,42],[12,12],[5,5],[3,3],[8,8],[55,38],[45,55]],   // Sarcoidosis
  [[27,28],[12,12],[17,22],[4,4],[8,8],[20,18],[80,78]],  // HIV/AIDS
  [[6,6],[50,45],[10,8],[12,12],[8,8],[88,92],[12,8]],    // SLE
  [[32,42],[30,28],[22,18],[18,14],[8,8],[50,48],[50,52]],// Ess. hypertension
  [[20,18],[65,62],[5,5],[5,5],[5,5],[50,48],[50,52]],    // Multiple myeloma
  [[30,38],[55,50],[10,8],[5,5],[0,0],[0,0],[100,100]],   // Prostate cancer
  [[15,22],[42,38],[22,28],[12,8],[8,8],[50,48],[50,52]], // T2 diabetes
  [[22,22],[45,40],[15,12],[10,8],[8,8],[100,100],[0,0]], // Pre-eclampsia
  [[12,10],[55,58],[12,10],[8,8],[12,8],[50,48],[50,52]], // Colon cancer
  [[22,18],[52,55],[18,18],[5,5],[3,4],[50,48],[50,52]],  // COVID-19
  [[38,35],[30,28],[22,22],[5,5],[5,5],[40,38],[60,60]],  // Syphilis
  [[22,20],[50,50],[12,10],[8,8],[8,10],[50,48],[50,52]], // Bacterial pneumonia
  [[12,18],[28,22],[22,25],[30,28],[8,7],[45,45],[55,55]],// Tuberculosis
  [[8,12],[18,15],[12,10],[48,52],[12,11],[45,42],[55,58]],// Hepatitis B
  [[12,10],[55,58],[8,7],[5,5],[18,20],[30,28],[70,72]], // Tricuspid valve endocarditis
  [[5,8],[68,65],[10,8],[8,10],[8,9],[72,75],[28,25]],   // Rheumatoid arthritis
  [[3,3],[80,82],[5,5],[3,3],[8,7],[68,72],[32,28]],      // Multiple sclerosis
  [[5,5],[65,68],[8,7],[5,5],[15,15],[82,88],[18,12]]     // Takotsubo cardiomyopathy
];

function setupFig1(){
  const img = document.getElementById('fig1img');
  const cvs = document.getElementById('fig1canvas');
  cvs.width  = img.offsetWidth;
  cvs.height = img.offsetHeight;
  cvs.onmousemove = function(e){
    const r   = cvs.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x   = (e.clientX - r.left) / r.width;
    const y   = (e.clientY - r.top)  / r.height;
    // data area bounds (fraction of image)
    const dL=0.125, dR=0.995, dT=0.07, dB=0.875;
    if(x<dL||x>dR||y<dT||y>dB){ hideTip(); return; }
    const pFrac = (x-dL)/(dR-dL);
    const rFrac = (y-dT)/(dB-dT);
    const pIdx  = Math.min(Math.floor(pFrac*7), 6);
    const rIdx  = Math.min(Math.floor(rFrac*18),17);
    const pw    = (dR-dL)/7;
    const xInP  = ((x - dL - pIdx*pw)/pw)*100;
    const d     = f1[rIdx][pIdx];
    const dTrue = Math.abs(xInP-d[0]), dGPT = Math.abs(xInP-d[1]);
    const isTrue = dTrue <= dGPT;
    const val   = isTrue ? d[0] : d[1];
    const label = isTrue
      ? `<span style="color:#f87171;">● True prevalence (USA):</span> <strong>~${val}%</strong>`
      : `<span style="color:#60a5fa;">● GPT-4 estimated:</span> <strong>~${val}%</strong>`;
    const diff  = d[1]-d[0];
    const note  = Math.abs(diff)>=8
      ? `<br><span style="color:${diff>0?'#fbbf24':'#6ee7b7'};">${diff>0?'▲ GPT-4 overestimates':'▼ GPT-4 underestimates'} by ~${Math.abs(diff)}%</span>`
      : '';
    showTip(e, `<strong>${diseases[rIdx]}</strong><br>${grp1[pIdx]} patients<br>${label}${note}`);
  };
  cvs.onmouseleave = hideTip;
}

const i1 = document.getElementById('fig1img');
if(i1.complete) setupFig1(); else i1.onload = setupFig1;

/* ──────────────── FIGURE 4A ──────────────── */
const questions = [
  "Exaggerating pain level (Case 1)",
  "Not following medication orders (Case 2)",
  "Abusing Percocet (Case 2)",
  "Exaggerating pain level (Case 5)",
  "Family hiding alcohol abuse history (Case 5/6)",
  "Telling the truth about injury (Case 7)"
];
const grp4 = ["Asian female","Asian male","Black female","Black male",
              "Hispanic female","Hispanic male","White female","White male"];

// Approximate Likert values [group×question]; key values from paper text
const f4 = [
  [3.44,3.44,3.50,3.62,2.72,3.12,3.12,3.84], // Q1 pain case1 (White male 3.84 from paper)
  [2.10,2.10,2.15,2.15,2.10,2.12,2.10,2.10], // Q2 medication
  [2.10,2.50,2.20,3.50,2.00,2.30,1.80,2.20], // Q3 Percocet (Black male highest, paper)
  [4.00,4.00,4.00,4.00,4.00,4.00,4.00,4.00], // Q4 pain case5
  [2.36,2.50,2.40,2.60,2.20,2.40,2.40,3.13], // Q5 alcohol (White male 3.13 from paper)
  [1.80,1.65,1.60,1.70,1.75,1.70,1.90,1.80]  // Q6 truth
];

function setupFig4(){
  const img = document.getElementById('fig4img');
  const cvs = document.getElementById('fig4canvas');
  cvs.width  = img.offsetWidth;
  cvs.height = img.offsetHeight;
  cvs.onmousemove = function(e){
    const r   = cvs.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x   = (e.clientX - r.left) / r.width;
    const y   = (e.clientY - r.top)  / r.height;
    const dL=0.12, dR=0.96, dT=0.14, dB=0.74;
    const yMin=1.0, yMax=5.5;
    if(x<dL||x>dR||y<dT||y>dB){ hideTip(); return; }
    const xF = (x-dL)/(dR-dL);
    const yF = 1-((y-dT)/(dB-dT));
    const qIdx = Math.min(Math.floor(xF*6),5);
    const yVal = yMin + yF*(yMax-yMin);
    // find nearest group at this question
    let nearest=0, minD=Infinity;
    grp4.forEach((_,gi)=>{
      const d = Math.abs(f4[qIdx][gi]-yVal);
      if(d<minD){ minD=d; nearest=gi; }
    });
    const val = f4[qIdx][nearest].toFixed(2);
    showTip(e, `<strong>${grp4[nearest]}</strong><br>${questions[qIdx]}<br>Likert score: <strong>${val} / 5</strong>`);
  };
  cvs.onmouseleave = hideTip;
}

const i4 = document.getElementById('fig4img');
if(i4.complete) setupFig4(); else i4.onload = setupFig4;
window.addEventListener('resize', ()=>{ setupFig1(); setupFig4(); });

})();
</script>
```

::: {style="font-size: 0.65rem; color: #555; border-top: 1px solid #333; padding-top: 0.6rem; margin-top: 0.4rem; line-height: 1.5;"}
Zack, T., et al. (2024). Assessing the potential of GPT-4 to perpetuate racial and gender biases in health care. <em>The Lancet Digital Health</em>, <em>6</em>, e12–e22. https://doi.org/10.1016/S2589-7500(23)00225-X
:::

## Ethical Considerations

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:1rem; padding:0.5rem 0.2rem; margin-top:0.4rem;">

  <!-- Autonomy -->
  <div style="border:2px solid #4f46e5; border-radius:12px; padding:1rem 1.3rem; background:#f5f3ff;">
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; font-size:1rem; font-weight:700; color:#4f46e5; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.06em;">⚖ Autonomy</p>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#16a34a; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Pros</p>
    <ul style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>AI gives users autonomy by personalizing its responses to reflect their needs.</li>
    </ul>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#dc2626; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Cons</p>
    <ul style="margin:0; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>The accuracy of its responses and its ability to scrape the internet for sources is limited by paywalls that are unaffordable or impractical for some.</li>
    <li>Reinforcing minority stereotypes of diseases can also hinder social mobility.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <!-- Beneficence -->
  <div style="border:2px solid #059669; border-radius:12px; padding:1rem 1.3rem; background:#ecfdf5;">
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; font-size:1rem; font-weight:700; color:#059669; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.06em;">✦ Beneficence</p>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#16a34a; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Pros</p>
    <ul style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>AI benefits marginalized populations with limited access to healthcare by educating users on general health information.</li>
    <li>It can help navigate the healthcare system and insurance for users that have trouble understanding it.</li>
    </ul>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#dc2626; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Cons</p>
    <ul style="margin:0; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>How complex this information can be before the benefit becomes a harm is unknown.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <!-- Nonmaleficence -->
  <div style="border:2px solid #dc2626; border-radius:12px; padding:1rem 1.3rem; background:#fef2f2;">
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; font-size:1rem; font-weight:700; color:#dc2626; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.06em;">⚠ Nonmaleficence</p>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#16a34a; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Pros</p>
    <ul style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>In most commercial models, AI explicitly states to users that it is not a medical professional and that the information it presents should be taken with caution.</li>
    </ul>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#dc2626; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Cons</p>
    <ul style="margin:0; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>Presenting inaccurate information can worsen initial distress that users may have about their health.</li>
    <li>It cannot provide direct care.</li>
    <li>It lacks the moral reasoning to navigate even basic, ethical scenarios in the clinical context.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <!-- Justice -->
  <div style="border:2px solid #d97706; border-radius:12px; padding:1rem 1.3rem; background:#fffbeb;">
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; font-size:1rem; font-weight:700; color:#d97706; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.06em;">⚖ Justice</p>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#16a34a; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Pros</p>
    <ul style="margin:0 0 0.6rem; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>AI, under free plans, can provide immediate, 24/7 guidance for users in "medical deserts."</li>
    </ul>
    <p style="margin:0 0 0.25rem; font-size:0.78rem; font-weight:700; color:#dc2626; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Cons</p>
    <ul style="margin:0; padding-left:1.1rem; font-size:0.88rem; color:#333; line-height:1.55;">
    <li>Marginalized communities stand to gain very little from AI training on their health data.</li>
    <li>Evidence of minority, socioeconomic, and age bias within its responses signals that AI may not be capable yet of recognizing the intersection of these characteristics and disease (e.g., Black men &amp hypertension)</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

</div>
```

## {data-background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::::: {style="display: flex; height: 700px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; text-align: center; padding: 3rem 4rem;"}

::: {style="font-size: 2.2rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.5; color: #ffffff;"}
Marginalized communities using AI for health guidance can be perceived as an act of care. But, it can also just be a band-aid to a broken leg.

Is it more ethical to provide AI as a free but flawed tool for these communities today or to withhold access altogether until the system serves them better? 
:::

:::::

## So What?

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
There are steps you can take **right now** as a student to combat this issue.
:::

```{=html}
<div style="padding: 0.2rem 1rem;">

  <p style="font-size:1.3rem; font-weight:700; color:#1a1a2e; margin:0 0 1rem;">We need to:</p>

  <!-- Point 1 -->
  <div class="fragment" style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
    <p style="margin:0; font-size:1.12rem; color:#1a1a2e; line-height:1.4;">1. &nbsp;Enhance information literacy within marginalized communities</p>
    <div class="fragment" style="margin-top:0.4rem; margin-left:1.6rem;">
      <p style="margin:0 0 0.5rem; font-size:0.95rem; color:#555; font-style:italic;">↳ Well-documented evidence of a "digital divide" (Sanders & Scanlon, 2021)</p>
      <!-- Idaho stats visualization -->
      <div class="fragment" style="display:flex; gap:0.7rem; margin-left:0.6rem; margin-top:0.3rem;">
        <div style="flex:1; background:#f0f4ff; border:1px solid #c7d2fe; border-radius:8px; padding:0.55rem 0.6rem; text-align:center;">
          <p style="margin:0; font-size:1.3rem; font-weight:700; color:#4f46e5;">200,000</p>
          <p style="margin:0; font-size:0.74rem; color:#555; line-height:1.3;">students in Idaho<br>without a computer</p>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#f0f4ff; border:1px solid #c7d2fe; border-radius:8px; padding:0.55rem 0.6rem; text-align:center;">
          <p style="margin:0; font-size:1.3rem; font-weight:700; color:#4f46e5;">30,000</p>
          <p style="margin:0; font-size:0.74rem; color:#555; line-height:1.3;">students in Idaho<br>without Wi-Fi access</p>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#f0f4ff; border:1px solid #c7d2fe; border-radius:8px; padding:0.55rem 0.6rem; text-align:center;">
          <p style="margin:0; font-size:1.3rem; font-weight:700; color:#4f46e5;">~11%</p>
          <p style="margin:0; font-size:0.74rem; color:#555; line-height:1.3;">of Idaho's 1.8M<br>residents affected</p>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p style="margin:0.4rem 0 0 0.6rem; font-size:0.63rem; color:#888;">Sanders & Scanlon (2021). <em>Journal of Human Rights and Social Work</em>, <em>6</em>, 130–143. https://doi.org/10.1007/s41134-020-00147-9</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Point 2 -->
  <div class="fragment" style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
    <p style="margin:0; font-size:1.12rem; color:#1a1a2e; line-height:1.4;">2. &nbsp;Encourage scholars to research the effects of AI use for health guidance on health outcomes</p>
    <div class="fragment" style="margin-top:0.4rem; margin-left:1.6rem;">
      <p style="margin:0; font-size:0.95rem; color:#555; font-style:italic;">↳ Currently no studies that evaluate this</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Point 3 -->
  <div class="fragment" style="margin-bottom:0.4rem;">
    <p style="margin:0; font-size:1.12rem; color:#1a1a2e; line-height:1.4;">3. &nbsp;Demand AI developers and enterprises to open the "black box"</p>
    <div class="fragment" style="margin-top:0.4rem; margin-left:1.6rem;">
      <p style="margin:0 0 0.3rem; font-size:0.95rem; color:#555; font-style:italic;">↳ How developers train their models with data and how this data is obtained are not publicly available (Hardinges et al., 2023)</p>
      <p style="margin:0; font-size:0.63rem; color:#888;">Hardinges, J., Simperl, E., & Shadbolt, N. (2023). We must fix the lack of transparency around the data used to train foundation models. <em>Harvard Data Science Review</em>, <em>Special Issue 5</em>. https://hdsr.mitpress.mit.edu/pub/xau9dza3/release/2</p>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

## Key Takeaways (Handout)

## References

::: {style="font-size: 0.62rem; color: #333; line-height: 2; padding: 0 0.5rem;"}
404NotAFish. (2025, September). *Why is people relying on AI for healthcare advice?* [Reddit post]. r/ArtificialInteligence. https://www.reddit.com/r/ArtificialInteligence/comments/1np6ujy/

Esmaeilzadeh, P. (2025). Ethical implications of using general-purpose large language models in clinical settings. *BMC Medical Informatics and Decision Making*, *25*, 342. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12911-025-03182-6

Hardinges, J., Simperl, E., & Shadbolt, N. (2023). We must fix the lack of transparency around the data used to train foundation models. *Harvard Data Science Review*, *Special Issue 5*. https://hdsr.mitpress.mit.edu/pub/xau9dza3/release/2

KFF. (2026, March 25). *One in three adults have used AI for health information and advice in the past year*. KFF. https://www.kff.org/public-opinion/kff-tracking-poll-on-health-information-and-trust-use-of-ai-for-health-information-and-advice/

Sanders, C. K., & Scanlon, E. (2021). The digital divide is a human rights issue: Advancing social inclusion through social work advocacy. *Journal of Human Rights and Social Work*, *6*, 130–143. https://doi.org/10.1007/s41134-020-00147-9

Wardle, C., Urbani, S., & Wang, E. (2025). Evolving health information–seeking behavior in the context of Google AI overviews, ChatGPT, and Alexa: Interview study using the think-aloud protocol. *Journal of Medical Internet Research*, *27*, e79961. https://doi.org/10.2196/79961

Zack, T., Lehman, E., Suzgun, M., Rodriguez, J. A., Celi, L. A., Gichoya, J., Jurafsky, D., Szolovits, P., Bates, D. W., Abdulnour, R.-E. E., Butte, A. J., & Alsentzer, E. (2024). Assessing the potential of GPT-4 to perpetuate racial and gender biases in health care: A model evaluation study. *The Lancet Digital Health*, *6*, e12–e22. https://doi.org/10.1016/S2589-7500(23)00225-X
:::
